title: Kafka Producers Deep Dive
learningObjectives: Tune **acks**, **retries**, and **delivery.timeout.ms** with clear durability semantics
Use **idempotent** producers and explain duplicate handling vs consumer idempotency
Diagnose **metadata** issues and **buffer** exhaustion from producer metrics
Present a coherent **min ISR** + **RF** story for payment-grade topics

sections.title: Why Kafka Producers Deep Dive matters
sections.content:
**Producers** decide **how** events **enter** the **log**: **durability**, **latency**, and **duplicate** behavior **start** here. Senior interviews probe **acks**, **idempotence**, **batching**, and **transactional** trade-offs with **numbers**, not slogans.

Misconfigured **retries** and **ISR** policies cause **duplicate** downstream **effects** or **silent** **durability** gaps—tie fixes to **metrics** and **topic** **policy**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Producers Deep Dive
sections.content:
### Day 60 — **Kafka** **producers**

### 1. Durability triangle
**acks**, **retries**, **delivery.timeout.ms**, and **min.insync.replicas** must be reasoned **together**.

### 2. Idempotence
**PID** + **sequence** numbers **dedupe** producer **retries** at the protocol.

### 3. Throughput tuning
**linger.ms**, **batch.size**, **compression**, **buffer.memory**.

### 4. Ordering pitfalls
**in-flight** + **retries** can **reorder** unless constrained.

### 5. Transactions
For **read-process-write** EOS patterns—latency and **operational** complexity rise.

### 6. Story
**Brownout** + **disabled** idempotence → **finance** duplicates; fix **idempotence** + **outbox** consumers.

sections.title: Basic — Producer vocabulary
sections.output:
acks=all waits on ISR policy
enable.idempotence dedupes producer retries
linger.ms trades latency for batching

sections.title: Intermediate — Effective batch size estimate
sections.description: Toy: records per batch from total bytes and avg record size.

sections.title: Advanced — Retry budget toy
sections.description: Cap attempts with a simple retry loop budget.

sections.title: Producer to leader and ISR
sections.description: Producer batches; replicas acknowledge per acks policy.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 60 — Producer path
Producer -> Leader : produce request
Leader -> ISRfollowers : replicate
ISRfollowers --> Leader : caught up
Leader --> Producer : ack per acks config
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Kafka producers — durability trio (Day 60 assignment)
Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What does **acks=all** mean?
conceptual.answer: Producer waits for **all** **in-sync replicas** to **ack** according to **broker** **ISR** policy before **success**—pairs with **min.insync.replicas** for **real** durability. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Kafka producers**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Producer waits for **all** **in-sync replicas** to **ack** according to **broker** **ISR** policy be Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Producer waits for **all** **in-sync replicas** to **ack** according to **broker** **ISR** Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

conceptual.question: Why **enable.idempotence=true**?
conceptual.answer: Producer **dedupes** **retries** with **sequence** numbers so **network** blips **do not** create **distinct** duplicate **records** at the broker **layer**. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

conceptual.answer: Upper bound for **send** attempts including **retries**—after it **fails** the record **unless** you handle **exceptions**. wrongAnswers: **Idempotence** removes need for **consumer** **dedupe** — **Correction:** **Still** need **business** **idempotency** for **side effects**.
**Transactional** producer is default — **Correction:** **Overhead**; use when **EOS** story **requires** it.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Knob | Effect |
|---|---|
| acks | Durability vs latency |
| idempotence | Retry-safe protocol dedupe |
| retries | Recovery vs storm risk |
| linger/batch | Throughput vs latency |
| buffer.memory | Backpressure surface |
| compression | Bandwidth vs CPU |
| delivery.timeout | Hard cap on attempts |
| transactional.id | EOS sessions fencing |
| max.in.flight | Ordering vs throughput |
| partitioner | Key to partition routing |
